name: Likeke Falls
tagline: A short jungle scramble to a waterfall that feels way more remote than it actually is.
parking: Park at the Ko'olau Golf Club lot off Pali Highway — the trail starts behind the maintenance area
bestTime: After rain for the best water flow — morning for fewer people
talkStory: About 2 miles round trip with 400 ft elevation change. The trail is short but scrappy — expect muddy sections, root tangles, and a couple stream crossings. The 15-ft waterfall at the end drops into a small pool surrounded by mossy rocks and jungle. Not as dramatic as Manoa Falls but way more private. You can connect this to the Old Pali Road trail for a longer loop. Wear shoes with grip and long pants — the overgrowth brushes against you the whole way.
